summary: Iteration 10 verified the strict-classification overhaul: WRatio case fix (processor=rf_utils.default_process), Rule C (ref + supporting debtor>=85 + fully consumed => FULL), Pass 2.5 (token-substring fallback as debtor_tokens/low confidence), and distinctive_tokens() helper. 13/14 new iter10 tests PASS + 18/18 prior regression suites (bearer auth + auth refresh + strict classification) PASS. One GENUINE BACKEND BUG found: Rule B does not exclude method='debtor_tokens', so Pass 2.5 token-substring matches with partial_token_set_ratio=100 can be promoted to FULL/high — directly violating the spec 'Never promotes to FULL'. Frontend UI (Confirmed/Suggested tabs, Why matched? column, AlertTriangle, score%) already verified in iteration 9 and unchanged this iteration; backend-only changes.

critical.issue: Rule B includes debtor_tokens method via debtor_methods = [m for m in ms if m['method'] in ('debtor_name','debtor_tokens')]. Pass 2.5 uses partial_token_set_ratio which returns 100 trivially when 2 distinctive tokens are substrings of the bank text. Consequence: a noisy bank row like 'XX9988 RANDOM NOISE FORD CIVIL JOBNO 12-A' with a single debtor_tokens link (score=100, ambiguous=false, exact amount) wrongly reaches FULL/high. Spec explicitly states debtor_tokens 'never promotes to FULL'. FIX: Rule B should require debtor_methods[0]['method'] == 'debtor_name' (or exclude debtor_tokens from debtor_methods used by Rule B). Same goes for Rule C — currently Rule C also allows debtor_tokens score>=85 to trigger FULL when paired with a reference match.

action_items: FIX (HIGH): In /app/backend/server.py at the strict-classification block (around line 524 for Rule B and line 534 for Rule C), restrict the debtor-method gate to method=='debtor_name' so that debtor_tokens (Pass 2.5) never satisfies Rule B/C — e.g. `debtor_name_methods = [m for m in ms if m['method'] == 'debtor_name']` and use this in Rule B/C preconditions, while keeping the broader debtor_methods list for the PARTIAL reason construction.

Suggested test to add post-fix: bank row with only debtor_tokens link at score=100 + exact amount must be PARTIAL/low (already encoded in test_iter10_overhaul.test_pass_2_5_debtor_tokens_low_confidence).
critical_code_review_comments: WRatio case-fix is correctly applied at server.py:430 — verified WRatio('GLOBEX CORP','Globex Corporation', processor=rf_utils.default_process) >= 90 (unit test passes).
Rule C logic is correct in shape and the EMMERSON multi-invoice case reaches FULL with reason 'Reference 2008666 confirmed by debtor name (95.7%) and exact amount' — end-to-end verified.
distinctive_tokens() correctly filters CORP_SUFFIXES + STOP_TOKENS + <4-char tokens; all unit tests pass.

Pass 2.5 is structurally correct (skips when remaining<=0, requires >=2 distinctive tokens, marks method=debtor_tokens / confidence=low / proper reason) — but the downstream Rule B/C does NOT respect the 'never FULL' guarantee. The bug is in the classification step, not in Pass 2.5 itself.
Match line `score%` display would hide score 0 due to truthy check (`m.score ? ` ${m.score}%` : ''`). Cosmetic only — score is never 0 above threshold.
INVOICE_REF_PATTERN requires >=3 digits, which is reasonable, but worth documenting since references like 'INV-T02' (2 digits) silently bypass Pass 1.
server.py is now 1114 lines — getting close to the 700-line guideline; consider splitting matching engine into a separate module.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iter10_overhaul.py (NEW — 14 tests covering WRatio fix, Rule C, Pass 2.5, distinctive_tokens, full 6-row canonical scenario)

success_rate.backend: 93% (13/14 new iter10 + 18/18 prior regression = 31/32 PASS; the 1 failure is the genuine Rule B bug)
success_rate.frontend: Not re-tested this iteration — backend-only changes; iteration 9 visual verification still applies (Confirmed/Suggested tabs, Why matched? column, AlertTriangle, score%, stat labels)

seed_data_creation: ~14 transient TEST_iter10_* allocations created during pytest via POST /api/allocations under admin user. Not cleaned up — isolated to admin user, no leakage to other tenants.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All iter10 backend pieces work EXCEPT the Rule B/C debtor_tokens leak. After the main agent restricts Rule B/C to method='debtor_name' only, re-run pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iter10_overhaul.py — test_pass_2_5_debtor_tokens_low_confidence should then pass. Frontend AllocationDetail.jsx + Dashboard.jsx unchanged this iteration; iteration 9 confirmed UI good. WRatio case fix verified via both unit test and HTTP integration. Rule C 'EMMERSON' canonical case verified FULL/high. Pass 2.5 surfaces low-confidence token-substring matches as expected; skips when bank already consumed; requires >=2 distinctive tokens (verified). distinctive_tokens helper filters CORP_SUFFIXES and STOP_TOKENS as designed.

rule_b_debtor_tokens_leak.problem: Pass 2.5 was specced to 'never promote to FULL', and the link itself is correctly marked confidence='low'. However the final classification's Rule B uses debtor_methods which includes method 'debtor_tokens' alongside 'debtor_name'. Because Pass 2.5 uses partial_token_set_ratio (substring-based) which trivially scores 100 when tokens appear verbatim, a single debtor_tokens link with exact amount and ambiguous=false passes Rule B's score>=95 + non-ambiguous + len(ms)==1 + fully_consumed gate. Result: noisy token-substring matches incorrectly become FULL/high, undermining the finance-grade conservatism.
rule_b_debtor_tokens_leak.reproduction: Bank row 'XX9988 RANDOM NOISE FORD CIVIL JOBNO 12-A' £5400, single invoice 'Ford Civil Engineering Construction Ltd' £5400. Run /api/allocations. Result: status='full', confidence='high', matches=[{method:'debtor_tokens', score:100, ambiguous:false, confidence:'low'}] — should be PARTIAL/low.
rule_b_debtor_tokens_leak.mitigation: In server.py change Rule B (line ~524) and Rule C (line ~534) preconditions: replace `debtor_methods` with a stricter `debtor_name_methods = [m for m in ms if m['method'] == 'debtor_name']` for those rules, leaving the broader debtor_methods for the PARTIAL-branch reason text. ~3 lines.
